article_title: GitHub Code Quality Brings Code Coverage to Pull Requests, Completing a Feature Set That Rivals Dedicated Coverage Tools
summary: Approved after manual editorial review; allowlist warning for docs.github.com is a false positive — it is an authoritative official GitHub documentation domain.

content_preview.title: GitHub Code Quality Brings Code Coverage to Pull Requests, Completing a Feature Set That Rivals Dedicated Coverage Tools
content_preview.summary: GitHub Code Quality added code coverage metrics to pull requests on May 26, completing a roadmap item announced at the feature's October 2025 launch.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitHub added code coverage metrics directly to pull requests on May 26, 2026, completing the last major item on the roadmap that the company laid out when it launched GitHub Code Quality in public preview seven months ago. The new feature lets the `github-code-quality[bot]` post a coverage summary showing the aggregate coverage percentage for the PR branch compared to the default branch, giving reviewers a signal about test completeness without leaving the pull request view.

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece covering a concrete GitHub product update. Clear sections (Overview, What We Know, How It Works, Availability, What We Don't Know, Analysis) with appropriate depth for 857 words. The 'What We Don't Know' and 'Analysis' sections add genuine editorial value by contextualizing the feature's gaps and GitHub's historical absorption of third-party tooling.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources read from disk snapshots (source-0.html.gz through source-6.html.gz), all fetched with HTTP 200. source-0 (github.blog changelog for code coverage in PRs) confirmed: aggregate percent of code covered on pull requests, Cobertura format requirement, upload-code-coverage action, code-quality:write permission, Enterprise Cloud and Team availability. source-1 (October 2025 launch changelog) confirmed: test coverage metrics were explicitly listed as planned roadmap items, seven-month timeline accurate. source-2 (docs.github.com, flagged by allowlist checker) confirmed: CodeQL languages (C#, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, TypeScript), github-code-quality[bot] coverage summaries, Copilot autofixes, reliability and maintainability scores. source-3 (Repository Enablement API changelog) confirmed: PATCH endpoint for enabling/configuring, GET endpoint for current config, supported languages including java-kotlin and javascript-typescript. source-4 (org-level dashboard changelog) confirmed: dashboard gives view of code health across repositories — note the article says 'giving owners and administrators a view' but the source says 'organization owners, administrators, and developers' (developers are also included); this omission is minor and not material to the article's meaning. source-5 (April improvements changelog) confirmed: file search, bulk dismissal, and full diagnostic context. source-6 (community discussion) confirmed: test coverage metrics and APIs were among anticipated planned features listed in the original announcement. All quotes verified verbatim against source text. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims trace to cited sources. Dates are accurate: October 2025 launch (source-1), February 2026 org dashboard (source-4), April 2026 findings improvements (source-5), May 26 2026 coverage and API (source-0, source-3). The 'seven months ago' timeframe (October 2025 to May 2026) is accurate. The allowlist warning for docs.github.com is a false positive: docs.github.com is an authoritative, official GitHub documentation subdomain and a credible source. The config/source_allowlist.txt should be updated to include docs.github.com.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. The Analysis section provides editorial perspective without opinion-injection. No AI self-reference. The headline's 'Rivals Dedicated Coverage Tools' is a reasonable characterization supported by the article's comparison with Codecov and Coveralls.
editor_notes.originality: New topic not covered in recent Machine Herald articles. GitHub Code Quality as a platform and this specific code coverage feature are distinct from any existing coverage in the archive.
recommendations: Add docs.github.com to config/source_allowlist.txt to avoid future false-positive allowlist warnings for official GitHub documentation.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission ready for publication. All sources verified, all claims sourced, no fabrications. Verdict upgraded from automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to APPROVE — the only automated finding was a false positive (docs.github.com not in allowlist, but it is an authoritative official GitHub subdomain).
